title: n8n credentials unavailable in manual execution mode
symptom: When manually executing an n8n workflow, nodes that require OAuth credentials fail with 'credentials not available' or 'OAuth credentials unavailable'.
applies_when: Node uses OAuth-based credentials (HubSpot, Google, etc.)
not_this_if: Credentials are not configured at all
root_cause: n8n's manual execution mode runs in a different context than production. OAuth credentials that were authorized in the main process may not be accessible in the manual execution sandbox. This is especially common after n8n restarts or when using external credential stores.
fix_steps: Open the credential in n8n Settings > Credentials
Save the credential, then retry manual execution
If still failing, try activating the workflow and triggering via webhook instead of manual execution
agent_instruction: Re-authenticate the OAuth credential in n8n Settings > Credentials. If manual execution still fails, activate the workflow and trigger via webhook instead.
safety_notes: Never expose OAuth tokens in workflow JSON exports
Do not log credential IDs in error reports
version_notes: Verify n8n execution mode and credential access method
